steps.title: Support the advantage claim
steps.content: Whitlock and Harmon conclude that delayed subfreezing temperatures advantage invasive species. The strongest support would show the invasive L. densiflorum benefiting more than the native O. secunda when those delays occur. Leaf production later into autumn is the concrete benefit because it extends the growing period before freezing conditions arrive.

steps.title: Compare native and invasive plants
steps.content: A shows the invasive stopping leaf production sooner, which weakens the claim. C adds early spring onset as another possible cause. D says neither species changed with temperature variation. The support is strongest when the same delayed-freeze condition helps both plants but helps the invasive much more. A much greater autumn extension for the invasive directly supports the conclusion. The answer is B.
